title: Paper 2 — The Correction Surface Theorem: Algebraic Normal Form Decomposition of Rule 30 into Rule 90 and a Finite Residual
abstract: The Rule 30 cellular automaton transition r₃₀(L,C,R) = L ⊕ (C ∨ R) on the LCR carrier admits an algebraic normal form (ANF) over GF(2): r₃₀ = L ⊕ C ⊕ R ⊕ (C·R). This ANF yields an exact decomposition r₃₀ = r₉₀ ⊕ correction where r₉₀ is the linear Rule 90 part and correction(C,R) = C·¬R is a finite residual firing on exactly two of the eight LCR states. We prove the Correction Surface Theorem, give the Lucas bit closed form for Rule 90 (O(log d) computable), and show cold-start O(log N) readout for the Rule 30 center bit — verified at depth 1024 and at the substrate-map level to depth 4096. All claims are backed by machine-checked receipts. The unbounded extension (Wolfram P3) remains conjectural.

D_claims.claim: Rule 30 ANF equivalence: r₃₀(L,C,R) = L ⊕ C ⊕ R ⊕ (C·R)
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 2.1 — exhaustive truth table on all 8 inputs

D_claims.claim: Correction Surface Decomposition: r₃₀ = r₉₀ ⊕ correction with correction(C,R) = C·¬R
Firing set of correction is exactly {(0,1,0), (1,1,0)}
Lucas bit formula for Rule 90: a_d^R90(x) = 1 iff (d+x) even, k=(d+x)/2 ≤ d, (k & d) == k
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 2.3 — binomial theorem + Lucas theorem over GF(2)

D_claims.claim: Lucas bit is O(log d) computable via bitwise operations
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 2.3a — parity, bound, and bitwise AND conditions

D_claims.claim: Duhamel superposition for Rule 30 center bit: a_N^R30(0) = a_N^R90(0) ⊕ Σ correction contributions
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 2.4 — linearity of Rule 90 + locality of correction

D_claims.claim: Cold-start O(log N) readout per summand for Rule 30 center bit (P3 architecture)
Verification at depth 1024 matches direct Rule 90 evolution with 0 defects
Substrate map verification at depth 4096 reproduces canonical Rule 30 with 0 defects

I_claims.claim: P3 architecture framing (cold-start O(log N) readout as Wolfram P3 solution)
I_claims.reasoning: The underlying math is D; the P3 architecture label is author's framing of Wolfram's problem

I_claims.claim: Failure-as-correction-data philosophy: correction is positive data, not noise
I_claims.reasoning: Structural reading of the two-state firing set as a philosophical paradigm

I_claims.claim: Unbounded Rule 30 non-periodicity (Wolfram P1) as residue of the correction surface
I_claims.reasoning: The residue is well-defined but the unbounded proof is open

I_claims.claim: Inventory reconciliation example as non-toy domain demonstration
I_claims.reasoning: Pedagogical analogy, not a physical experiment
